buginformation.summary: [formatting] Add formatting setting for whitespace in permits clause
Unnecessary compliance parsing in batch compiler
buginformation.description:
Just stumbled over the compiler code in /org.eclipse.jdt.core/batch/org/eclipse/jdt/internal/compiler/batch/Main.java that used 16 instead of 17 compliance, but that was even not a problem because whole block was not needed after bug 562473.
I will push a gerrit.

buginformation.summary: inner Type not found in .class file.
buginformation.description:
Inner types have binary names containing a "$" while the search looks for "." separated qualified names.
java/util.Map$Entry vs java/util.Map.Entry
The "$"can not be simply textual replaced by "." since "$" is a valid Classname itself i.e. not necessary a separator.

buginformation.summary: Compile error shown when working with records though everything runs fine
buginformation.description:
Created attachment 287261 Example source
In our code we have a record, with 3 arguments, that implements an Interface. This interface provides static helper methods to create record instances with varying arguments. And as far as I could not track down these static helper methods cause eclipse to show an error in the record file for 3 of the 6 constructors: "The body of a canonical constructor must not contain an explicit constructor call"
I attached an example. I tried to simply it more, but couldn't figure out the core issue.

buginformation.summary: [memory] deduplicate ClasspathAccessRule.pattern
buginformation.description:
Created attachment 287263 ClasspathAccessRule.pattern Screenshot.png
In our workspaces with ~ 500 connected plugin projects almost all of eclipses memory is used by org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.env.AccessRule[](Totaly 2.5 GB retained memory). It should be possible to either reuse equal AccesRules between different projects or at least to deduplicate the char[] pattern in ClasspathAccessRule. Which for us should reduce the memory usage by almost the number of projects which uses the same dependencies. As far as i understand PDE does automatically create the access rules based on manifest.

buginformation.summary: [compiler] ecj compiler error on code accepted by javac for generic varargs parameters
Record specific error needs to be thrown when a Class tries to extend a Record
NPE in Index.query(Index.java:152) on deleting project
buginformation.description:
Created attachment 287281 Eclipse debug call stack and eclipse error log
This error happens while my plugin is running.
My plugin performs these steps
- creates a java project - performs its task - deletes the java project created below
It seems to me which the error occurs during the delete of the java project.
The exception is: java.lang.NullPointerException at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.index.Index.query(Index.java:152) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.index.Index.getMetaIndexQualifications(Index.java:243) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.indexing.IndexManager$MetaIndexUpdateRequest.execute(IndexManager.java:1772) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.processing.JobManager.run(JobManager.java:432) at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:834)

buginformation.summary: Wrong compiler error reported for generic instanceof pattern matching
Java Search does not find any TYPE IMPLEMENTORS in .class files
buginformation.description:
I am trying to solve search issues in .class files. I do not know if exactly this issue was reported in any of the many bug reports for search, so i create a new ticket:
When i search for an implementor of a class and they are in a .class in a .jar without sourcecode attached then there are no hits. For both modular and non-modular. While there are hits when i search for the same thing in the sourcecode project.
As far as i see the issue is that the parameter IJavaSearchConstants.IMPLEMENTORS is converted to SuperTypeReferencePattern.ONLY_SUPER_INTERFACES and that does currently find strictly only explicit implemented interfaces that are listed in the class. The superclass is also implicit a implemented interface.
(Even "Object" would be - which is never explicitly listed, but i wont search for that.)
Note that IJavaSearchConstants.IMPLEMENTORS is defined as "The search result is a type that implements an interface *or extends a class*"

buginformation.summary: [performance] wake up sleeping indexer when needed
buginformation.description:
For example JavaSearchBugs9Tests spends most of its time in JobManager's sleep(500).
Patch performance can be testet with JavaSearchBugs9Tests. From 185sec down to 30sec on my computer.

buginformation.summary: Slow and huge workspace save
buginformation.description:
When i close eclipse in our ~500 plugins workspace i end up with large "state.dat" files in .metadata\.plugins\org.eclipse.core.resources\.projects\*\org.eclipse.jdt.core all together ~2GB big.
Reminds me of the similar amount of memory used in Bug 576458 (deduplicate ClasspathAccessRule.pattern). Since jdt deduplicates the ClasspathAccessRule in RAM i ask if we could do so on disk too please.
Most time (6 seconds) is spend in org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.builder.State.writeRestriction ()
May be related to Bug 575445 (Slow java project bulk close).

buginformation.summary: Useless warning in compact constructor of a record
Few warnings in jdt core
buginformation.description: I see few warnings in jdt.core in my workspace.

buginformation.summary: IllegalArgumentException when hovering over anonymous class
[18][switch pattern] - pattern dominating constant case label rule changes
buginformation.description:
Investigate the delta:
JEP 420 - Section 14.11.1 A switch label that has a pattern case label element p dominates another switch label that has a constant case label element c if p dominates c.
A pattern p dominates a constant c if one of the following is true:
p is a type pattern and either of the following is true:
the type of c is a primitive type and its wrapper class (5.1.7) is a subtype of the erasure of the type of p.
the type of c is a reference type and is a subtype of the erasure of the type of p.
JEP 406 (First Preview) - Section 14.11.1
A switch label that has a pattern case label element p dominates another switch label that has a constant case label element c if either of the following is true:
the type of c is a primitive type and its wrapper class (5.1.7) is a subtype of the erasure of the type of p. the type of c is a reference type and is a subtype of the erasure of the type of p.

buginformation.summary: [18][switch pattern] Applicability of T
buginformation.description:
Addition in Section 14.11.1
JEP 420:
A class or interface C is applicable at type T if one of the following holds:
Class or interface C is not generic and there is a casting conversion from T to C (5.5).
Class or interface C is generic and there is a casting conversion from T to the type C(T1, ..., Tn) where T1, ..., Tn_ are the type parameters of the class or interface C .

buginformation.summary: [18][switch pattern] Resolving A Switch Block
buginformation.description:
Sec 14.11.1 - Delta pertaining to resolving switch block
This paragraph has been expanded in JEP 420.
JEP 420 says, Resolving a switch block at type T results in an identical switch block except where every switch label L is replaced with the result of resolving L at type T.
Resolving a switch label L at type T proceeds by resolving all the default and case labels it uses as follows:
If L uses a default label then the resolved switch label uses a default label.
If L uses a case label then the resolved switch label uses a case label with the case label elements determined as follows:
All constant case label elements appearing in L appear in the resolved switch label.
All default case label elements appearing in L appear in the resolved switch label.
If a null case label element and no pattern case element labels appear in L then a null case label element appears in the resolved switch label.
If no null case label element appears in L then for every pattern case label element p appearing in L, a pattern case element label q appears in the resolved switch label, where q is the result of resolving pattern p at type T.
The definition of resolving a pattern at a type is given in 14.30.2.
If a null case label element appears in L then for every pattern case label element appearing in L that is a type pattern declaring a pattern variable x of type U, a pattern case element q appears in the resolved switch label, where q is an any pattern that declares x of type U.
All pattern case element labels appearing in a switch label that also contains a null case element label are required to be type patterns (14.11.1).
This final rule covers an important aspect of resolving a switch label. If a switch label has both null and type pattern case label elements, then these are resolved to a single pattern case label element that is an any pattern. This ensures that the initialization of the pattern variable declared by the type pattern is always handled by the semantics of pattern matching, even when the value being matched is null.
while JEP 406 says: A switch block is resolved at type U resulting in a identical switch block except where every pattern case label element p is replaced with a pattern case label element whose pattern is the result of resolving pattern p at type U.
Feel free to create child bugs as deemed necessary

buginformation.summary: [warnings] Warning 'undocumented empty block' should be deactivated for Java records
Use try-with-resource if possible and StringWriter instead of ByteArrayOutputStream
buginformation.description: In JDT core, there are a couple of places, where the code could be simplified with try-with-resources pattern, and with using StringWriter instead of writing into a ByteArrayOutputStream and convert the result byte array into a String.

buginformation.summary: [18][switch pattern][test] enhanced switch statement cannot have an empty switch block
buginformation.description: JEP 420 Section 14.11.2 has an addition compared to that in JEP 406 "One consequence of requiring exhaustiveness is that an enhanced switch statement, unlike a normal switch statement, can not have an empty switch block." There should be a test case already testing this but since there is an explicit statement in spec, bug create for checking and if not adding a test case

buginformation.summary: Internal compiler error when switch expression returns a functional interface
buginformation.description:
Consider the following piece of code: ========== import java.util.ArrayList; import java.util.Comparator;
public class Hello { public static void main(String[] args) { String someSwitchCondition = null; new ArrayList(Long)().stream().sorted(switch (someSwitchCondition) { case "some_long" -) Comparator.comparingLong(Long::longValue); default -) throw new IllegalArgumentException("Unsupported"); }); } } ==========
An internal compiler error will be thrown with the following stack trace: ========== java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot read field "selector" because "method" is null at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.FunctionalExpression$1BridgeCollector.(init)(FunctionalExpression.java:331) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.FunctionalExpression.getRequiredBridges(FunctionalExpression.java:386) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.FunctionalExpression.requiresBridges(FunctionalExpression.java:389) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.ReferenceExpression.shouldGenerateImplicitLambda(ReferenceExpression.java:281) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.ReferenceExpression.generateCode(ReferenceExpression.java:319) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.Statement.generateArguments(Statement.java:443) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.MessageSend.generateCode(MessageSend.java:549) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.YieldStatement.generateCode(YieldStatement.java:190) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.SwitchStatement.statementGenerateCode(SwitchStatement.java:713) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.SwitchExpression.statementGenerateCode(SwitchExpression.java:235) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.SwitchStatement.generateCodeForStringSwitch(SwitchStatement.java:425) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.SwitchStatement.generateCode(SwitchStatement.java:487) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.SwitchExpression.generateCode(SwitchExpression.java:330) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.Statement.generateArguments(Statement.java:443) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.MessageSend.generateCode(MessageSend.java:549) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.Expression.generateCode(Expression.java:767) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.AbstractMethodDeclaration.generateCode(AbstractMethodDeclaration.java:354) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.AbstractMethodDeclaration.generateCode(AbstractMethodDeclaration.java:291) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.TypeDeclaration.generateCode(TypeDeclaration.java:761) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.TypeDeclaration.generateCode(TypeDeclaration.java:831) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.CompilationUnitDeclaration.generateCode(CompilationUnitDeclaration.java:412) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.dom.CompilationUnitResolver.resolve(CompilationUnitResolver.java:1246) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.dom.CompilationUnitResolver.resolve(CompilationUnitResolver.java:712) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.dom.ASTParser.internalCreateAST(ASTParser.java:1245) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.dom.ASTParser.createAST(ASTParser.java:868) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.manipulation.CoreASTProvider$1.run(CoreASTProvider.java:272) at org.eclipse.core.runtime.SafeRunner.run(SafeRunner.java:45) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.manipulation.CoreASTProvider.createAST(CoreASTProvider.java:264) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.manipulation.CoreASTProvider.getAST(CoreASTProvider.java:197) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.manipulation.SharedASTProviderCore.getAST(SharedASTProviderCore.java:138) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.ui.viewsupport.SelectionListenerWithASTManager$PartListenerGroup.calculateASTandInform(SelectionListenerWithASTManager.java:166) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.ui.viewsupport.SelectionListenerWithASTManager$PartListenerGroup$1.run(SelectionListenerWithASTManager.java:151) at org.eclipse.core.internal.jobs.Worker.run(Worker.java:63) ==========
Note that this is a Java 17 project and I'm using the latest JDT nightly build at the time of writing, 3.18.1000.v20211022-1800.

buginformation.summary: StackOverflowError in JavaModelManager.getArchiveValidity
buginformation.description:
No steps to reproduce yet. This happens on 4.21. I suspect it's something with an archive on the classpath.
!ENTRY org.eclipse.core.jobs 4 2 2021-10-26 15:17:58.563 !MESSAGE An internal error occurred during: "Java Search". !STACK 0 java.lang.StackOverflowError at java.base/java.util.HashMap.getNode(HashMap.java:567) at java.base/java.util.HashMap.get(HashMap.java:552) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.JavaModelManager.getArchiveValidity(JavaModelManager.java:3392) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.JavaModelManager.getArchiveValidity(JavaModelManager.java:3413) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.JavaModelManager.getArchiveValidity(JavaModelManager.java:3413) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.JavaModelManager.getArchiveValidity(JavaModelManager.java:3413) ...

buginformation.summary: [18] Create Java 18 version and supporting code in tests
buginformation.description:
Requirement as follows:
1) Profile configuration in JDT core source for running in level 18 2) Supporting code in tests 3) Required command line options
See bug 572864

buginformation.summary: [formatter] Switch expression formatting broken in a method chain with lambdas
[releng] Files with CR-LF as line endings
buginformation.description: Four Java source files in JDT core use CR-LF as line endings. I suggest to normalize them to LF.

buginformation.summary: [formatter] Wrong indentation for method invocation on multi-line expression
buginformation.description:
Hi. I've tried to modify every possible indent and line wrap setting in the Eclipse Java formatter settings and I think this is a bug.
I have this source test: ``` when(identityService.getByValueFromReader(transactionBeneficiary.getIdentity().getType(), transactionBeneficiary.getIdentity().getValue(), organization.getCompanyId(), organization.getOrganizationId())) .thenReturn(identity); ```
That I expect to format as: ``` when( identityService.getByValueFromReader( transactionBeneficiary.getIdentity().getType(), transactionBeneficiary.getIdentity().getValue(), organization.getCompanyId(), organization.getOrganizationId())) .thenReturn(identity); ```
But it ends up indenting the last line too far: ``` when( identityService.getByValueFromReader( transactionBeneficiary.getIdentity().getType(), transactionBeneficiary.getIdentity().getValue(), organization.getCompanyId(), organization.getOrganizationId())) .thenReturn(identity); ```
Not that it should matter, this works correctly in IntelliJ.
Thanks.

buginformation.summary: [9] "More Concise try-with-resources Statements" feature missing
[compiler] StackOverflowError with invalid code passed as module info

Method Signature error with compareAndSet()
Exhaustive switch expressions is reported to be not exhaustive if one of the subtypes is sealed itself

[pattern instanceof] Pattern instanceof causes Open Declaration to fail.
[18] Merge from master to BETA_JAVA18 Periodically
buginformation.description: Merge periodically from master to BETA_JAVA18

buginformation.summary: NPE in EclipseCompiler when passing empty classes iterable
[18] Release Activities
buginformation.description:
Top Level for bug general release activities:
- Increase the bundle service version This must not use the same version as the base. As always, we increase the service segment by 50 for the beta work ==) 3.x. 50
(e.g. 0 -) 50, 1 -) 50, 100 -) 150).
Tasks: 1. Disclaimers to be removed in Beta Branch 2. Y build after Disclaimer removal 3. Merge Beta to master 4. Updating Marketplace Entry 5. Sending mail to community with MPE

buginformation.summary: [18] Remove JCP disclaimers from code, UI and marketplace
buginformation.description: Remove JCP disclaimers from code, UI and marketplace

buginformation.summary: [content assist] no completions on lambda variables when the lambda is nested
[completions] no completions on stream operations without a prefix after the first operation.
buginformation.description:
Sample Code:
Stream.of("1").map(Long::valueOf).map(|)
The second map operation doesn't get any completions unless a prefix is used or ending with a semi-colon.
This is same if you try like this
Stream.of("1").map(|).map(Long::valueOf)
But in this case even if you have a semi-colon it will not provide completions until you remove the following operations from the statement. But adding a prefix provide completions.

buginformation.summary: [18] JSR 269 API Changes for Java 18
buginformation.description:
The following changes have been made/proposed to the JSR 269 APIs. We need to investigate whether or not JDT needs to do something to adopt.
JDK-8267631: Add SourceVersion.RELEASE_18 JDK-8273157: Add convenience methods to Messager JDK-8275368: Correct statement of kinds of elements Processor.process operates over JDK-8140442: Add getOutermostTypeElement to javax.lang.model utility class JDK-8275308: Add valueOf(Runtime.Version) factory to SourceVersion JDK-8276772: Refine javax.lang.model docs JDK-8224922: Access JavaFileObject from Element(s) JDK-8277303: Terminology mismatch between JLS17-3.9 and SE17's javax.lang.model.SourceVersion method specs JDK-8277522: Make formatting of null consistent in Elements

buginformation.summary: [18][javadoc] provide @snippet support in javadoc for inline snippets
[18][javadoc] provide @snippet support in javadoc for External snippets, including @start, @end and region
[18][javadoc] provide support for @highlight markup in @snippet
buginformation.description:
@highlight markup can be used to highlight a specific substring in a snippet in javadoc
https://openjdk.java.net/jeps/413 has examples

buginformation.summary: [18][javadoc] provide support for @replace markup in @snippet
buginformation.description:
@replace markup can be used to modify the display text in a snippet in javadoc
https://openjdk.java.net/jeps/413 has examples

buginformation.summary: [18][javadoc] provide support for @link markup in @snippet
buginformation.description:
@link markup can be used to linkthe display text in a snippet to declarations elsewhere in the API, in javadoc
https://openjdk.java.net/jeps/413 has examples

buginformation.summary: [18][javadoc] provide support for regions in @snippet in @highlight, @replace, or @link tags
buginformation.description:
Regions are optionally-named ranges of lines that identify the text to be displayed by a snippet.
The beginning of a region is marked by either
@start region=name, or an @highlight, @replace, or @link tag that specifies region or region=name. You can omit the name if it is not needed by the matching @end tag.
This bug is to provide support for regions for @highlight @link and @replace tags.

buginformation.summary: [formatter] Case statement in switch expression is not line wrapped
ModularClassFile#exists is time consuming for Jar without module-info.class
buginformation.description:
ModularClassFile#exists is time consuming for Jar without module-info.class.
There is a cache in JavaModelManager#getInfo but it's not caching not existing module-info.class.

buginformation.summary: [18][switch pattern] non-abstract sealed class switch expression type of a non-exhaustive switch accepted incorrectly
[18][switch pattern] - Exhaustive switch with type variable T with upper bound on abstract sealed class flagged error
[content assist] no constructor completions inside lambda
[18][switch pattern] compilation error for case with guarded pattern with a boolean variable
[18][switch pattern] Wrong output printed with guarded pattern
[completions] no completions on method arguments if the invocation inside a lambda
OOM in HashtableOfObject: Requested array size exceeds VM limit
buginformation.description:
Since yesterday I constantly see following error in my main SDK workspace. I can rebuild index, restart, do some work and it will appear again, tried multiple times. I do not see related changes, so I assume the problem is not a recent regression (see bug 474323), but might be related to the increased index size (I've added one or two additional JDK installations, might be that is it).
Two things: 1) I can't see which file causes it, I can't check this file 2) May be a trivial size check could be added?
java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Requested array size exceeds VM limit at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.util.HashtableOfObject.(init)(HashtableOfObject.java:41) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.index.DiskIndex.readCategoryTable(DiskIndex.java:679) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.index.DiskIndex.mergeCategory(DiskIndex.java:483) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.index.DiskIndex.mergeCategories(DiskIndex.java:475) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.index.DiskIndex.mergeWith(DiskIndex.java:577) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.index.Index.save(Index.java:225) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.indexing.IndexManager.saveIndex(IndexManager.java:1139) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.indexing.IndexManager.saveIndexes(IndexManager.java:1192) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.indexing.IndexManager.notifyIdle(IndexManager.java:818) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.processing.JobManager.run(JobManager.java:422) at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:834)

buginformation.summary: [18] Eclipse reports wrong syntax error with switch pattern nested with another switch expression
[18] DOM AST support for JEP 413
buginformation.description: All the DOM AST support for JEP 413 will be handled via this bug.

buginformation.summary: [compiler] Unhandled event loop exception in AST while hovering over method (ClassCastException)
[18] DOM AST support for JEP 420
buginformation.description: To handle the DOM AST support for JEP 420

buginformation.summary: Wrong null analysis detection when accessing a field without `this.` prefix
[formatter] Keep braced code on one line: settings for new switch constructs
buginformation.description: Switch cases/expression with arrow operator are a good candidate to implement the "Keep braced code on one line" formatter settings for.

buginformation.summary: [18][javadoc] provide proper error reporting support for snippets
buginformation.description:
for the below snippet
{@snippet : classA() }
The error after javadoc generation is - invalid snippet
Similar error needs to be reported with proper error message.

buginformation.summary: [18][javadoc] provide content assist support for annotations within snippet
buginformation.description: Content assist support needs to be provided within snippets for @highlight, @replace , @link.

buginformation.summary: [18][javadoc] provide support for property values to be wrapped in single quotes for @highlight in snippets
buginformation.description:
Currently there is support to have property values to be string literals for @highlight in snippets, but as per https://openjdk.java.net/jeps/413
the property values of substring, regex and type can be specified in single quotes
substring="abc" is supported but substring='abc' also needs to be supported

buginformation.summary: [18] Bug in computing enum constant value in switch when a pattern is present
[18] Pattern variable reported as uninitialized when mixing case pattern with instanceof pattern
[18] Mixing String constants with pattern variables produces wrong output
[18] Case pattern takes precedence over an enum constant
[18] VerifyError when guarded pattern contains an AND_AND expression
[18][javadoc] support single line comments within comment in Snippet for @replace
[18][javadoc] provide support for multiple annotations within @snippet
buginformation.description:
provide support for multiple annotations within single line comments in @snippet
The below scenario
/** * {@snippet: * System.out.println(); // @replace regex="out" replacement="in" @highlight substring="println" * } */
only highlight happens and @replace is ignored. Both annotations have to work on the string .
so the snippet should be
System.in.println
with println in bold.

buginformation.summary: [18] Pattern variables used in guard expression are reported when they are not final
[18] Bogus error about switch not completing normally
[18] [DOM AST]Support for Regions and Multiple tags on Text element in JavaDoc from DOM
buginformation.description: Bug 578523 requires support for DOM to be able to store and access multiple tags for a text line Bug 578041 requires the support of regions inside JavaDoc

buginformation.summary: [18] Compiler ignores condition of a guarded pattern in an instanceof expression
[18] Super interface of the switch expression type is not considered to be total pattern
Compiler wrongly parses text block escape sequences
AIOOBE in TypeDeclarationPattern$PackageNameSet.rehash
buginformation.description:
Created attachment 288019 Screenshot
An error popped up. I think I was opening the Search Type (CMD+SHIFT+T) action:
java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeConstructorAccessorImpl.newInstance0(Native Method) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeConstructorAccessorImpl.newInstance(NativeConstructorAccessorImpl.java:62) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingConstructorAccessorImpl.newInstance(DelegatingConstructorAccessorImpl.java:45) at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Constructor.newInstance(Constructor.java:490) at java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: Index 2929 out of bounds for length 1461 at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.matching.TypeDeclarationPattern$PackageNameSet.rehash(TypeDeclarationPattern.java:84) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.matching.TypeDeclarationPattern$PackageNameSet.add(TypeDeclarationPattern.java:76) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.matching.TypeDeclarationPattern.decodeIndexKey(TypeDeclarationPattern.java:228) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.search.SearchPattern.findIndexMatches(SearchPattern.java:2517) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.search.SearchPattern.findIndexMatches(SearchPattern.java:2479) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.matching.MatchLocator.findIndexMatches(MatchLocator.java:306) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.PatternSearchJob.search(PatternSearchJob.java:238) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.PatternSearchJob.search(PatternSearchJob.java:212) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.PatternSearchJob.lambda$0(PatternSearchJob.java:148) at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinTask$AdaptedCallable.exec(ForkJoinTask.java:1448) at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinTask.doExec(ForkJoinTask.java:290) at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinPool$WorkQueue.topLevelExec(ForkJoinPool.java:1020) at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinPool.scan(ForkJoinPool.java:1656) at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinPool.runWorker(ForkJoinPool.java:1594) at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinWorkerThread.run(ForkJoinWorkerThread.java:177)

buginformation.summary: [null][external] broken internal data flows for EEA
buginformation.description:
While configuring a set of real world projects to leverage bug 488494, bug 509397 and bug 574603 I found some data flows where information about external annotations was dropped in name environments:
NameEnvironment.computeClasspathLocations(..) passes allLocationsForEEA into some newly created ClasspathLocations, but not all. Notably this is missing when calling ClasspathLocation.forLibrary() where target is a java.io.File (i.e., an external jar).
SearchableEnvironment.createAnswer(..) uses the wrong format of packageName when passed into new ExternalAnnotationProvider(..): we are using a '.' separated (as suitable for findPackageFragments()), which then doesn't match the '/' separated name in the EEA-header. An exception will let the type appear unannotated.

buginformation.summary: "Failed to save JDT index" error on deleted index
buginformation.description:
Just saw this error in our automated tests that create / delete projects very often.
I guess we shouldn't save index if it was deleted in the meantime.
!ENTRY org.eclipse.jdt.core 4 4 2022-02-10 11:11:25.530 !MESSAGE Failed to save JDT index: Index for /74act299 !STACK 0 java.io.FileNotFoundException: /tmp/aloskuto_ws-e4x-r731ly/.metadata/.plugins/org.eclipse.jdt.core/3882635292.index (No such file or directory) at java.base/java.io.FileInputStream.open0(Native Method) at java.base/java.io.FileInputStream.open(FileInputStream.java:219) at java.base/java.io.FileInputStream.(init)(FileInputStream.java:157) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.index.FileIndexLocation.getInputStream(FileIndexLocation.java:81) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.index.DiskIndex.readCategoryTable(DiskIndex.java:669) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.index.DiskIndex.mergeCategory(DiskIndex.java:485) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.index.DiskIndex.mergeCategories(DiskIndex.java:477) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.index.DiskIndex.mergeWith(DiskIndex.java:579) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.index.Index.save(Index.java:225) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.indexing.IndexManager.saveIndex(IndexManager.java:1139) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.indexing.IndexManager.saveIndexes(IndexManager.java:1192) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.indexing.IndexManager.notifyIdle(IndexManager.java:818) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.processing.JobManager.run(JobManager.java:422) at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:834)

buginformation.summary: [content assist] Provide concrete types when expecting an interface on new object completion
[code completion] skip the ignored types before creating CompletionProposals in CompletionEngine
buginformation.description:
Here are the steps on how the JDT responds to the completion request when typing 'List list = new A|'.
1. query indexes to find all possible constructor names with the typed chars 'A'. 2. CompletionEngine accepts the index query results. If the proposal kind is not ignored by the CompletionRequestor, then create CompletionProposal (with relevance calculated). 3. CompletionRequestor accepts the CompletionProposals and applies type filters to discard the CompletionProposals from ignored types. 4. present completion proposals to users.
If we can ignore the filtered types earlier, for example, by doing it in step 2, as we did for ignored kind, it avoids computing the unused CompletionProposals. This is meaningful for constructor method completion performance. When the CompletionEngine creates constructor proposals, it relies on TypeBinding to compute the relevance of the proposed type. Resolving TypeBindings are a little expensive when they are not loaded before. Applying type filters earlier can help load fewer TypeBindings and improve performance.

buginformation.summary: NoSuchFileException in AddJarFileToIndex
buginformation.description:
During automatic tests we observed the following logged exception:
!ENTRY org.eclipse.jdt.core 4 4 2022-02-21 12:54:48.956 !MESSAGE Failed to index /tmp/someJar_11802131409544529548_ForTests.jar !STACK 0 java.nio.file.NoSuchFileException: /tmp/someJar_11802131409544529548_ForTests.jar at java.base/sun.nio.fs.UnixException.translateToIOException(UnixException.java:92) at java.base/sun.nio.fs.UnixException.rethrowAsIOException(UnixException.java:111) at java.base/sun.nio.fs.UnixException.rethrowAsIOException(UnixException.java:116) at java.base/sun.nio.fs.UnixFileAttributeViews$Basic.readAttributes(UnixFileAttributeViews.java:55) at java.base/sun.nio.fs.UnixFileSystemProvider.readAttributes(UnixFileSystemProvider.java:149) at java.base/sun.nio.fs.LinuxFileSystemProvider.readAttributes(LinuxFileSystemProvider.java:99) at java.base/java.nio.file.Files.readAttributes(Files.java:1764) at java.base/java.util.zip.ZipFile$Source.get(ZipFile.java:1239) at java.base/java.util.zip.ZipFile$CleanableResource.(init)(ZipFile.java:732) at java.base/java.util.zip.ZipFile$CleanableResource.get(ZipFile.java:849) at java.base/java.util.zip.ZipFile.(init)(ZipFile.java:247) at java.base/java.util.zip.ZipFile.(init)(ZipFile.java:177) at java.base/java.util.zip.ZipFile.(init)(ZipFile.java:191) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.indexing.AddJarFileToIndex.execute(AddJarFileToIndex.java:157) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.processing.JobManager.run(JobManager.java:441) at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:834)
If the jar file was deleted on disk while the indexing was running, we shouldn't log an error - the code in AddJarFileToIndex.execute() already handles the exception. Either ignoring the exception or logging an info would be fine.

buginformation.summary: [18][javadoc] provide support for reporting invalid snippets in javadoc
buginformation.description: Currently, if the snippet is invalid, this is not reported in the javadoc. This bug is to provide both the JDT Core and UI support for the same

buginformation.summary: Intermittent failures in CopyMoveElementsTests
JavaElementDeltaTests.testChangeJRE8_9 fails randomly
[18][javadoc] provide support for properties for @snippet
buginformation.description:
Provide support to specify properties for a snippet
such as class, file , id etc
Please look at https://openjdk.java.net/jeps/413#Other-kinds-of-files

buginformation.summary: IndexManager checks awaitingJobs size not in synchronized block
buginformation.description: IndexManager extends JobManager and while the later always accesses "awaitingJobs" in synchronized blocks, former doesn't do that. This is not correct, the access should be synchronized for MT data consistency. Beside that, there is already synchronized awaitingJobsCount() method that is used elsewhere and probably should be used here too. I will push a patch.

buginformation.summary: [18][javadoc] any character between single comment and annotation like @replace or @highlight makes annotation invalid
[18] [javadoc] in @snippet tag, tags in single line comments of previous line ending with : should be combined with tags with tags in next line when required
[18] Generated Javadoc includes the trailing brace
java indexer became slower
buginformation.description:
From 4.20 to 4.22 indexing our whole workspace became much slower I sampled a workspace during Preferences/java/"Rebuild Index" 4.20: ~670 sec 4.22: ~1.400 sec in org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.search.indexing.IndexManager$2.execute ()
the new hotspot is org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.builder.ClasspathJar.findPackageSet() during resolve()
It is not getting better with -Dorg.eclipse.jdt.disableMetaIndex=true but still i guess it is caused by the meta index (bug 570078) since that hotspot was already reported in Bug 577974, 576245 for 4.21

buginformation.summary: Indexer does sometimes not show progress in Progress View
buginformation.description:
the ProgressJob only show progress until currentJob() is null. Unfortunatly currentJob() does not return the current but the next job that would need to run. So during the last job the ProgressJob always terminates. =) the last job can never been see in Progress View.
More over a new ProgressJob is only scheduled if JobManager.progressJob==null but the termination of the ProgressJob does not reset progressJob to null. So after a last Job has been executed the index progress remains silent until eventually the currentJob() becomes null again... while during the time the last jobs runs a lot of other jobs can have been queued up. Especially if the last job is a job like IndexAllProject which spawns new jobs for every source file.

buginformation.summary: [18][javadoc] support identifiers as values in @snippet for @highlight @link and @replace
[18][pattern switch] OOM error in switch pattern - valid code
buginformation.description:
From bug 576746 comment 3
From Alexander Kriegisch -- reproduced below verbatim
------------------------------------------------------------
public class SwitchPatternPreview2OK { static void constantLabelMustAppearBeforePattern(Integer o) { switch (o) { case -1, 1 -) System.out.println("special case:" + o); case Integer i i ) 0 -) System.out.println("positive integer: " + o); case Integer i -) System.out.println("other integer: " + o); } } }
------------------------------------------------------------
I immediately got:
$ java -jar org.eclipse.jdt.core/target/org.eclipse.jdt.core-3.29.50-SNAPSHOT-batch-compiler.jar -18 --enable-preview ../AspectJ/tests/features199/java18/SwitchPatternPreview2OK.java Picked up _JAVA_OPTIONS: -Djava.net.preferIPv4Stack=true ---------- 1. ERROR in C:\Users\alexa\Documents\java-src\AspectJ\tests\features199\java18\SwitchPatternPreview2OK.java (at line 0) public class SwitchPatternPreview2OK { ^ Internal compiler error: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.codegen.BranchLabel.addForwardReference(BranchLabel.java:57) ---------- 2. WARNING in C:\Users\alexa\Documents\java-src\AspectJ\tests\features199\java18\SwitchPatternPreview2OK.java (at line 5) case Integer i i ) 0 -) System.out.println("positive integer: " + o); ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ You are using a preview language feature that may or may not be supported in a future release ---------- 3. WARNING in C:\Users\alexa\Documents\java-src\AspectJ\tests\features199\java18\SwitchPatternPreview2OK.java (at line 6) case Integer i -) System.out.println("other integer: " + o); ^^^^^^^^^ You are using a preview language feature that may or may not be supported in a future release ---------- Exception in thread "main" java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.codegen.BranchLabel.addForwardReference(BranchLabel.java:57) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.codegen.CaseLabel.branch(CaseLabel.java:35) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.codegen.CodeStream.tableswitch(CodeStream.java:7552) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.SwitchStatement.generateCode(SwitchStatement.java:563) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.AbstractMethodDeclaration.generateCode(AbstractMethodDeclaration.java:354) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.AbstractMethodDeclaration.generateCode(AbstractMethodDeclaration.java:291) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.TypeDeclaration.generateCode(TypeDeclaration.java:761) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.TypeDeclaration.generateCode(TypeDeclaration.java:831) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ast.CompilationUnitDeclaration.generateCode(CompilationUnitDeclaration.java:412) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.Compiler.process(Compiler.java:913) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.compiler.ProcessTaskManager.run(ProcessTaskManager.java:145) at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:833)
------------------------------------------------------------
Actually, originally I wanted to use some example code right from JEP 420, ...
public class SwitchPatternPreview2OK { static void constantLabelMustAppearBeforePattern(Object o) { switch (o) { case -1, 1 -) System.out.println("special case:" + o); case Integer i i ) 0 -) System.out.println("positive integer: " + o); case Integer i -) System.out.println("other integer: " + o); default -) System.out.println("non-integer: " + o); } } }
------------------------------------------------------------
but that yielded:
$ java -jar org.eclipse.jdt.core/target/org.eclipse.jdt.core-3.29.50-SNAPSHOT-batch-compiler.jar -18 --enable-preview ../AspectJ/tests/features199/java18/SwitchPatternPreview2OK.java Picked up _JAVA_OPTIONS: -Djava.net.preferIPv4Stack=true ---------- 1. ERROR in C:\Users\alexa\Documents\java-src\AspectJ\tests\features199\java18\SwitchPatternPreview2OK.java (at line 4) case -1, 1 -) System.out.println("special case:" + o); ^^ Type mismatch: cannot convert from int to Object ---------- 2. ERROR in C:\Users\alexa\Documents\java-src\AspectJ\tests\features199\java18\SwitchPatternPreview2OK.java (at line 4) case -1, 1 -) System.out.println("special case:" + o); ^ Type mismatch: cannot convert from int to Object ---------- 3. WARNING in C:\Users\alexa\Documents\java-src\AspectJ\tests\features199\java18\SwitchPatternPreview2OK.java (at line 5) case Integer i i ) 0 -) System.out.println("positive integer: " + o); ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ You are using a preview language feature that may or may not be supported in a future release ---------- 4. WARNING in C:\Users\alexa\Documents\java-src\AspectJ\tests\features199\java18\SwitchPatternPreview2OK.java (at line 6) case Integer i -) System.out.println("other integer: " + o); ^^^^^^^^^ You are using a preview language feature that may or may not be supported in a future release ---------- 4 problems (2 errors, 2 warnings)
------------------------------------------------------------
But Javac also does not seem to like the int constants with an Object switch type. I have no idea why they chose that example for the spec.
I re-tested this with the upstream beta 18 branch, no AspectJ mixed in at all, pure JDT Core. So before you merge, better double-check. But maybe I was just misunderstanding the mailing list discussion,and this is actually still under development, as the open ticket suggests.

buginformation.summary: CompilationUnitResolver.getProjectPath(IJavaProject) is not correct
further reduce jdt state save
buginformation.description: continue from Bug 576646, a few more bytes can be saved when writing Long int that do only require 3 bytes instead of 4.

buginformation.summary: [17] PreviewFeatureTest.test001 - 16 fails in all Java 17+ builds
buginformation.description:
https://download.eclipse.org/eclipse/downloads/drops4/I20220403-1800/testResults.php
https://download.eclipse.org/eclipse/downloads/drops4/I20220403-1800/testresults/html/org.eclipse.jdt.core.tests.compiler_ep424I-unit-cen64-gtk3-java18_linux.gtk.x86_64_18.html
https://download.eclipse.org/eclipse/downloads/drops4/I20220403-1800/testresults/html/org.eclipse.jdt.core.tests.compiler_ep424I-unit-macM1-java17_macosx.cocoa.aarch64_17.html
junit.framework.ComparisonFailure: Invalid problem log . ----------- Expected ------------ ----------
 1. ERROR in X.java (at line 3)
 Zork z = null;
 ^^^^
 Zork cannot be resolved to a type
 ----------
 2. WARNING in X.java (at line 5)
 return Modifier.SEALED;
 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
 You are using an API that is part of a preview feature and may be removed in future
 ----------
 3. WARNING in X.java (at line 8)
 return this.getClass().getPermittedSubclasses();
 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
 You are using an API that is part of a preview feature and may be removed in future
 ----------

------------ but was ------------ ----------
 1. ERROR in X.java (at line 3)
 Zork z = null;
 ^^^^
 Zork cannot be resolved to a type
 ----------

--------- Difference is ---------- expected:(...type
 ----------
 [2. WARNING in X.java (at line 5)
 return Modifier.SEALED;
 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
 You are using an API that is part of a preview feature and may be removed in future
 ----------
 3. WARNING in X.java (at line 8)
 return this.getClass().getPermittedSubclasses();
 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
 You are using an API that is part of a preview feature and may be removed in future
 ----------
 ]) but was:(...type
 ----------
 []) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.tests.junit.extension.TestCase.assertStringEquals(TestCase.java:266) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.tests.junit.extension.TestCase.assertEquals(TestCase.java:242) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.tests.compiler.regression.AbstractRegressionTest.checkCompilerLog(AbstractRegressionTest.java:1488) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.tests.compiler.regression.AbstractRegressionTest.runTest(AbstractRegressionTest.java:3490) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.tests.compiler.regression.AbstractRegressionTest.runTest(AbstractRegressionTest.java:3193) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.tests.compiler.regression.AbstractRegressionTest.runNegativeTest(AbstractRegressionTest.java:2874) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.tests.compiler.regression.AbstractRegressionTest.runNegativeTest(AbstractRegressionTest.java:2865) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.tests.compiler.regression.PreviewFeatureTest.test001(PreviewFeatureTest.java:59) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DirectMethodHandleAccessor.invoke(DirectMethodHandleAccessor.java:104) at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:577) at junit.framework.TestCase.runTest(TestCase.java:177) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.tests.junit.extension.TestCase.runTest(TestCase.java:969) at junit.framework.TestCase.runBare(TestCase.java:142) at junit.framework.TestResult$1.protect(TestResult.java:122) at junit.framework.TestResult.runProtected(TestResult.java:142) at junit.framework.TestResult.run(TestResult.java:125) at junit.framework.TestCase.run(TestCase.java:130) at junit.framework.TestSuite.runTest(TestSuite.java:241) at junit.framework.TestSuite.run(TestSuite.java:236) at junit.framework.TestSuite.runTest(TestSuite.java:241) at junit.framework.TestSuite.run(TestSuite.java:236) at org.eclipse.jdt.core.tests.util.CompilerTestSetup.run(CompilerTestSetup.java:59) at junit.framework.TestSuite.runTest(TestSuite.java:241)
